justification: All pass criteria met: PM/Legal contradiction explicitly identified and escalated as first escalation with detailed options. Clock-start ambiguity identified as dedicated escalation. Wrong-address exception from Finance noted as BR-6 and explored in edge cases. Auto-refund vs 3-day processing conflict noted as explicit escalation. 10 business rules extracted with correct invariant classifications. 5 formal escalations provided. Confidence level of 35 is well below the 50 threshold.

observations.text: PM/Legal guarantee vs best-effort contradiction is the lead escalation with three resolution options and a pragmatic recommendation (Option C 'Express Promise')
Clock-start ambiguity given its own dedicated escalation with clear options and customer-centric recommendation
Wrong-address exception captured as BR-6 and further explored in edge cases (interaction with auto-refund BR-9, determination process escalation)
Auto-refund vs 3-business-day processing conflict explicitly escalated with three resolution options
10 well-structured business rules extracted — exceeds the 5 minimum — with thoughtful invariant classifications and testable assertions
5 formal escalations covering contradiction, missing context, and ambiguity — exceeds the 2 minimum
Confidence at 35 with clear reasoning about why the contradictions prevent writing acceptance criteria
Acceptance criteria deliberately left empty with rejection reasons explaining why — shows disciplined refusal to fabricate completeness
Edge cases are excellent — identifies subtle interactions like driver cancellation at minute 115, auto-refund vs wrong-address conflict, and multiple driver cancellations
BR-4 driver rating correctly classified as invariant: false — recognizes the threshold is adjustable policy, not a physical constraint

BR-3 pickup SLA classified as invariant: true is debatable — while the justification argues it's a physical/time constraint, the 15-minute threshold itself is a policy choice (could be 12 or 20 minutes)
Rejection reasons clearly articulate why the domain model cannot proceed to implementation, tying back to specific business rules

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'contradiction', got values: 'missing_context', 'missing_context', 'ambiguity', 'missing_context'
